title: Cloud, Wi-Fi & Backup
summary: Cloud Connect (private cloud reach), Managed Wi-Fi (secure, scalable wireless), and 5G Wireless Internet Backup (continuity)—customer outcomes, discovery, and proof points aligned to GPC collateral.

sections.heading: Cloud Connect — customer outcomes
sections.body: Buyers want predictable performance to major clouds without building or maintaining their own long-haul network fabric. Outcomes to anchor: faster, more consistent access to SaaS and IaaS; lower latency vs best-effort Internet paths; security and control for traffic between sites and cloud regions; flexibility to connect to multiple providers (AWS, Azure, Google, Oracle, IBM, SAP, Nutanix, Salesforce, Alibaba) from GPC’s ecosystem; ability to add multi-region redundancy from a single interconnection mindset—supporting resilience without re-architecting everything on day one.

sections.heading: Cloud Connect — discovery
sections.body: Which clouds or SaaS platforms are business-critical today and what is planned in the next 12–24 months? How are they reaching those services today (DIA, VPN, colo, third-party cloud exchange)? Where do they feel latency, jitter, or capacity limits (e.g., backup windows, real-time apps, large data movement)? Do they need connectivity across metros or regions, and is redundancy a board-level or compliance driver? Who owns cloud networking and security architecture—IT, security, DevOps, or a partner? Any requirements for isolating or prioritizing traffic vs general Internet browsing?

sections.heading: Cloud Connect — proof points
sections.body: GPC Cloud Connect offers a direct, private path to leading cloud and network providers on GPC’s cloud ecosystem, paired with Managed Ethernet as the on-ramp—so customers access cloud content over a secure, private connection rather than only public Internet. Collateral highlights: no need for the customer to own or manage the physical network to the ecosystem; secure traffic between metros and regions; multi-region redundancy by connecting to multiple cloud regions from a single interconnection point; direct cloud connections with speeds up to 10 Gbps; reliable, predictable performance and efficiency through reduced latency vs many Internet-only designs. Transport footprint includes interconnection into GPC’s cloud ecosystem (e.g., Omaha, Chicago, Denver as shown on the product sheet).

sections.heading: Managed Wi-Fi — customer outcomes
sections.body: Organizations need wireless that matches how people work and visit: reliable coverage, separation of guest vs corporate traffic, and security that does not fall to an overstretched internal team. Outcomes: employees and guests stay productive; consistent experience across offices, education, healthcare, retail, and venues; lower total cost of ownership through a fully managed lifecycle (design, install, turn-up, maintenance); visibility into how Wi-Fi is used for planning and accountability; optional brand presence on guest access.

sections.heading: Managed Wi-Fi — discovery
sections.body: How many sites need Wi-Fi and what mix of indoor vs outdoor coverage? What user types (employees, students, patients, customers) and approximate concurrent devices? Do they need separate guest and private networks—and any compliance or policy constraints (HIPAA-style segmentation, PCI for guest Wi-Fi, etc.)? What throughput or application mix matters (video, VoIP, POS, e-learning)? Who operates the network today—in-house IT, MSP, or none—and what outages or support pain drove the conversation? Do they want analytics on usage and a branded captive portal for guests?

sections.heading: Managed Wi-Fi — proof points
sections.body: Managed Wi-Fi scales from small business to large environments with Internet-backed speeds from 10 Mbps to 2 Gbps per collateral. Engineering is positioned to fit current and future density and coverage. Integrated guest and private networks over a secure connection; indoor and outdoor options with multiple access-point choices. Security stack messaging: next-generation firewalls, advanced routing, intrusion detection and prevention, and multiple SSIDs. Full managed solution from installation through ongoing maintenance to reduce TCO and burden on internal IT. Industry-leading analytics and reporting for guest and employee usage; branded login portal capability.

sections.heading: 5G Wireless Internet Backup — customer outcomes
sections.body: When fiber or primary access fails, revenue and operations do not pause on schedule. Outcomes: maintain continuity during power events, fiber cuts, or provider issues; keep critical applications running with prioritization; operational peace of mind via proactive monitoring and support; optional extended runtime when power is lost; predictable commercial terms for backup usage (e.g., unlimited data without overage charges per product sheet).

sections.heading: 5G Wireless Internet Backup — discovery
sections.body: What happens today when primary Internet fails—manual failover, no backup, or an aging solution? Which applications must stay up (POS, ERP, voice, security systems, cloud apps)? Acceptable downtime in minutes vs hours? Who gets alerted and how? Any site constraints for installing an outdoor antenna or router placement? Interest in battery backup for extended outages? Confirm geography and expectations for wireless performance (5G varies by site; collateral notes speed factors and deprioritization after certain monthly usage thresholds).

sections.heading: 5G Wireless Internet Backup — proof points
sections.body: Automatic switch to a secure high-speed wireless path when primary service fails; automatic return when primary is restored. Prioritization for essential business applications. 24/7 Network Operations Center monitoring and management with alerts and support. Optional battery backup up to eight hours during power loss. Unlimited data with no overage charges per collateral; 5G speeds up to 100 Mbps and higher with location-dependent variability; professional configuration and installation including Cradlepoint router. Footnote-level honesty for sellers: wireless speeds depend on location, signal, tower proximity, and environment; stated tiered speeds after 12 GB / 20 GB / 30 GB of monthly usage and possible carrier slowdowns when the network is busy—set expectations in discovery.
